descriptions.description: The AN/APG-77 is an active electronically scanned array (AESA) fire-control radar developed for the F-22 Raptor. Designed by Northrop Grumman, it provides all-weather air-to-air and air-to-ground target acquisition. The radar's low-probability-of-intercept (LPI) design minimises emissions that could be detected by enemy radar warning receivers. The upgraded AN/APG-77(V)1 variant adds synthetic aperture radar (SAR) ground mapping and ground moving target indication (GMTI) modes.
descriptions.shortDescription: AESA fire-control radar for the F-22 Raptor with low-probability-of-intercept design.

descriptions.description: The APG-77 uses a gallium arsenide monolithic microwave integrated circuit (MMIC) transmit/receive (T/R) module array. The antenna is tilted slightly rearward to reduce frontal radar cross-section. The radar can change frequency more than 1,000 times per second to reduce interception probability. It has simultaneous multi-target track-while-scan capability in both air-to-air and air-to-ground modes. The radar's emissions can also be focused to overload enemy sensors, providing a rudimentary electronic attack capability.
descriptions.shortDescription: GaAs T/R module array with LPI frequency agility and electronic attack capability.
